items.title: Chipotle Isn't Looking to Compete With McDonald's on Price, and Why That Could Be a Costly Mistake
items.description: The company's growth rate has been falling drastically in recent years, and it might not improve anytime soon.

items.title: Assessing McDonald's (MCD) Valuation As The Big Arch Burger Extension Fuels Menu Momentum
items.description: McDonald's (MCD) is about to test investor appetite with its largest burger yet, the Big Arch Burger. It will roll out across the U.S. for a limited time after positive international reception. See our latest analysis for McDonald's. The Big Arch launch sits on top of a broader run of product news, from $1 McMuffin promotions to upbeat commentary around recent quarterly results and customer response to menu changes. Over the same period, McDonald’s has seen a 30 day share price return of...

items.title: One Source of Strength in the Markets Today: Tech Stocks
items.description: Nvidia, Palantir and long-suffering Intuit were among the biggest gainers Monday as investors set aside tech fears.  Investors have fretted in the first months of 2026 about whether the world's biggest tech companies are at risk of overspending on artificial intelligence or whether, conversely, the success of AI will displace more traditional software providers.  Among the hardest hit stocks was Intuit, which dropped to a one-year low beneath $359 on Feb. 24.

items.title: Warren Buffett once admitted that selling McDonald’s shares was ‘a very big mistake.’ Today, they’d be worth over $10 billion
items.description: Warren Buffett once dumped millions of McDonald’s shares—and it turned into a multibillion-dollar investing lesson in patience.

items.title: 2 Cash-Producing Stocks to Research Further and 1 We Avoid
items.description: While strong cash flow is a key indicator of stability, it doesn’t always translate to superior returns. Some cash-heavy businesses struggle with inefficient spending, slowing demand, or weak competitive positioning.
